summary:
• Russia faces a significant diesel export supply shock following Ukraine's attacks on its refineries, potentially tightening global refined product markets and supporting crude demand.

• China's potential return to active energy market participation could create a major geopolitical price shock through increased competition for crude supplies and shifts in trading patterns across the Western Hemisphere.

• Multiple supply disruption risks in both crude and refined products are emerging simultaneously—from Russian refinery damage to potential disputes over LNG project costs in Mozambique—creating compounding upside pressure on oil and product prices.
